note: 100% step-free on the ground floor/nave & museum; free entry for disabled visitors + companion via the Carrer de la Marina priority entrance; metro stop accessible. Towers are NOT accessible (lift up, stairs down; wheelchairs/strollers barred).

Gaudi hilltop park; steep, partly unpaved, with stairs and escalators. Free disabled entry. Enter from Carretera del Carmel (bus 24/H6) to cut the climb; still challenging with wheels/strollers, go with a companion.
Wide, flat, exceptionally wheelchair-friendly walkway from Plaça Catalunya to the port; smooth navigation, accessible shops/restaurants along it.
Historic medieval quarter; charming but narrow alleys and some uneven surfaces. Navigable but travel light; Barcelona Cathedral and hidden squares within.
Vibrant covered food market off La Rambla; flat entry but very crowded, tight aisles slow wheels and seniors. Sensory feast.
One of the most accessible beaches in the world: beach wheelchairs, accessible paths, showers/toilets, and volunteer assistance in season. Great for families.
Montjuic hill has accessible sights (Magic Fountain, museums) reachable by adapted taxi/bus, but the Montjuic cable car / St Sebastia gondola is NOT wide enough for wheelchairs.
Gaudi modernist house-museum; generally has lift access to floors and adapted route (confirm current). Popular, book ahead.
Gothic cathedral in the old quarter; main floor largely reachable but surrounded by cobbles and some steps; cloister access varies.
Large flat central park with a boating lake, playgrounds, the mammoth statue and Barcelona Zoo; easy for strollers and wheels, lots for kids.
